qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via DeregCallbackUri in AMF 3GPP access registration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a nil pointer dereference in DataChangeNotificationProcedure (notifier.go) via POST /sdm-subscriptions causing DoS. This finding describes SSRF via unvalidated DeregCallbackUri on PUT /nudm-uecm/v1/:ueId/registrations/amf-3gpp-access. Different endpoint, different root cause (missing URL validation vs nil pointer dereference), different impact (SSRF vs DoS crash), different vulnerability class.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a nil pointer dereference in DataChangeNotificationProcedure (notifier.go) triggered via POST /sdm-subscriptions, causing UDM service crash (DoS). The non_qualifying_vuln finding 'missing-auth-callback-endpoint' correctly identifies the same nil pointer dereference in notifier.go via /sdm-subscriptions but is not a qualifying_vuln. The sole qualifying_vuln finding (ssrf-via-dereg-callback-uri) does not match the advisory root cause: it describes SSRF via a different endpoint and different vulnerable code path (SendOnDeregistrationNotification, not DataChangeNotificationProcedure).
evidence: Advisory root cause: nil pointer dereference in DataChangeNotificationProcedure (notifier.go) via POST /sdm-subscriptions with path traversal sequences
missing-auth-callback-endpoint (non_qualifying_vuln) identifies the same nil pointer dereference at notifier.go:25-30 but cannot be selected
ssrf-via-dereg-callback-uri targets PUT /nudm-uecm/v1/:ueId/registrations/amf-3gpp-access via SendOnDeregistrationNotification (notifier.go:55), not DataChangeNotificationProcedure
